title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement codeofconduct@posit.co. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to glycoverse
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to glycoverse
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("glycoverse/glycoverse", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to glycoverse
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use Air apply style, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to glycoverse
text: Please note glycoverse project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

code: @Manual{,   title = {glycoverse: Easy Install and Load the `Glycoverse`},   author = {Bin Fu},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.2},   url = {https://glycoverse.github.io/glycoverse/}, }

title: Easy Install and Load the `Glycoverse`
text: Glyco-Omics Data Analysis Made Easy Tidyverse-style ecosystem glycobiologists turn raw MS data biological insights.

title: Easy Install and Load the `Glycoverse`
text: glycoverse comprehensive R ecosystem designed glycomics glycoproteomics data analysis. provides unified pipeline covers entire analytical workflow, data import cleaning statistical analysis visualization. also provides dedicated infrastructure glycan structure analysis.

title: Easy Install and Load the `Glycoverse`
code: library(glycoverse)  # Import results from Byonic and GlycoQuant exp <- read_byonic_pglycoquant("result.csv", sample_info = "sample_info.csv")  # Preprocess clean_exp <- auto_clean(exp)  # Derived trait analysis trait_exp <- derive_traits(clean_exp)  # Statistical analysis dea_res <- gly_anova(trait_exp)  # Use pipeline for a more concise workflow dea_res <- exp |>    auto_clean() |>    derive_traits() |>    gly_anova()

title: Easy Install and Load the `Glycoverse`
text: Ecosystem units focusing experimental data structures, automated cleaning, robust statistical calculation interactive downstream plotting.

title: Easy Install and Load the `Glycoverse`
text: Packages representing, parsing, matching, deriving drawing glycan structures traits.

title: Easy Install and Load the `Glycoverse`
text: Additional packages reference data, annotation, pathway context, enrichment full pipeline assembly.

title: Easy Install and Load the `Glycoverse`
text: repository contains glycoverse meta-package, provides convenient tools managing entire glycoverse ecosystem: One-command installation: Install core packages Package updates: Update glycoverse packages single function Situation report: Check status installed glycoverse packages

title: Easy Install and Load the `Glycoverse`
text: installs meta-package core packages: glyexp, glyread, glyclean, glystats, glyvis, glyrepr, glyparse, glymotif, glydet, glydraw. Troubleshooting: “Failed download” “403” errors usually indicate network issues r-universe rate limiting. Try switching network environments installing GitHub.

title: Easy Install and Load the `Glycoverse`
code: pak::repo_add(glycoverse = "https://glycoverse.r-universe.dev") pak::pkg_install("glymotif")  # Also installs dependencies: glyrepr, glyparse, glyexp

title: Easy Install and Load the `Glycoverse`
text: Prerequisite: install packages GitHub, ’ll need proper compilation tools installed system. means RTools Windows Xcode Command Line Tools macOS. Without , ’ll likely see “find tools necessary compile package” error. ’re unsure process, quick search “install R package GitHub” provide helpful context tools necessary. Installing GitHub little tricky. Package dependencies within glycoverse resolved assuming packages r-universe (CRAN Bioconductor). means running following code: Installing glyxxx GitHub way bypass R-universe entirely, dependencies still hosted . experiencing network issues R-universe, switching GitHub version glyxxx resolve problem. truely install glycoverse package GitHub (along dependencies), install one one following dependency tree: Note: Check DESCRIPTION file package find dependencies. Troubleshooting: Encountering “HTTP error 403”? usually means IP rate-limited GitHub. resolve , need configure Personal Access Token (PAT). Sign GitHub account. Install Git local machine. Generate PAT via GitHub settings. Configure PAT locally (e.g., using gitcreds R package). step--step guide, search “set GitHub PAT R.”
code: # Do NOT run: pak::pkg_install("glycoverse/glyxxx@*release")  # common practice to install a GitHub package pak::pkg_install("glyrepr")  # from CRAN pak::pkg_install("glyparse")  # from CRAN pak::pkg_install("glycoverse/glyexp@*release") pak::pkg_install("glycoverse/glydraw@*release") pak::pkg_install("glycoverse/glyread@*release") pak::pkg_install("glycoverse/glyclean@*release") pak::pkg_install("glycoverse/glystats@*release") pak::pkg_install("glycoverse/glymotif@*release") pak::pkg_install("glycoverse/glyvis@*release") pak::pkg_install("glycoverse/glydet@*release")  # The meta-package pak::pkg_install("glycoverse/glycoverse@*release")

title: Easy Install and Load the `Glycoverse`
text: glydb, glyanno, glyenzy, glyfun, glysmith installed separately:

title: Easy Install and Load the `Glycoverse`
text: Load core packages: Check installation:
code: library(glycoverse) #> ── Attaching core glycoverse packages ───────────────── glycoverse 0.3.1.9000 ── #> ✔ glyclean 0.14.1          ✔ glyparse 0.6.0       #> ✔ glydet   0.11.0          ✔ glyread  0.11.0      #> ✔ glydraw  0.4.0           ✔ glyrepr  0.12.0      #> ✔ glyexp   0.14.1          ✔ glystats 0.10.0.9000 #> ✔ glymotif 0.14.1          ✔ glyvis   0.6.0       #> ── Conflicts ───────────────────────────────────────── glycoverse_conflicts() ── #> ✖ glyclean::aggregate() masks stats::aggregate() #> ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors glycoverse_sitrep()

title: Easy Install and Load the `Glycoverse`
text: --box analysis: Start glyexp → glyread → glysmith systematic learning: Glycoproteomics Analysis Glycomics Analysis Omics: glyexp → glyread → glyclean → glystats → glyvis Structures: glyrepr → glyparse → glymotif → glydet → glydraw might also find glydb, glyanno, glyenzy, glyfun useful specific tasks.

Update glycoverse packages — glycoverse_update
text: checks r-universe glycoverse packages (optionally, dependencies) reports ones newer versions available. can choose update automatically get manual instructions.

title: Update glycoverse packages — glycoverse_update
code: glycoverse_update(   recursive = FALSE,   repos = getOption("repos"),   dev_to_latest = NULL )

title: Update glycoverse packages — glycoverse_update
text: recursive TRUE, also list dependencies glycoverse packages. repos repositories use check updates. Defaults getOption("repos"). dev_to_latest TRUE, replace development versions (versions 4th component >= 9000, e.g., 0.1.0.9000) latest release versions. FALSE, keep development versions -. NULL (default), interactive mode, user prompted. non-interactive mode, NULL defaults FALSE.

title: glycoverse 0.3.2
text: glycomics glycoproteomics case studies now live standalone Glycoverse Tutorials website. (#6)

title: glycoverse 0.3.1
text: glyfun now managed non-core glycoverse package.

title: glycoverse 0.3.0
text: glycoverse_update() gains dev_to_latest parameter control whether development versions updated latest versions (#2).

title: glycoverse 0.2.5
text: Migrate GitHub releases r-universe package installation. glycoverse_update() now uses pak::repo_add() pak::pkg_install() automatic updates.

title: glycoverse 0.2.5
text: Remove Remotes field DESCRIPTION dependencies now installed r-universe. Update installation guide README.

title: glycoverse 0.2.0
text: glycoverse_sitrep() checks glycoverse packages now, including new packages, .e., glydraw, glydb, glyanno, glysmith. Remove glyenzy glycoverse core packages. glycoverse_update() now updates glycoverse packages using pak, instead just printing commands.

title: glycoverse 0.2.0
text: Fix bug glycoverse_deps() raised error recursive TRUE. Fix bug upstream version numbers Bioconductor dependencies fetched. Update dependencies latest versions. Add packages end case study vignettes.
